title: Probability
summary: 9709 P5 probability — sample space, combined events, independence, and tree diagrams with GeoGebra probability explorer.
learningObjectives: Calculate probabilities using addition and multiplication rules.
Determine whether events are mutually exclusive or independent.
Construct and use tree diagrams for multi-stage experiments.
Apply conditional probability P(A|B) = P(A ∩ B)/P(B).

simpleExplanation.title: Counting what might happen
simpleExplanation.summary: Probability quantifies uncertainty on a 0–1 scale. Combine rules carefully: 'or' uses addition (if exclusive), 'and' uses multiplication (if independent).
steps: P(A) between 0 and 1; P(not A) = 1 − P(A).
Mutually exclusive: P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B).
Independent: P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B).
Tree diagrams: multiply along branches, add disjoint outcomes.

sections.content: **Probability** on P5 underpins **discrete distributions** (5.4) and **normal approximation** (5.5). Tree diagrams are the safest method for **conditional** exam questions.

sections.question: P(rain) = 0.3 each day, independent. Find P(dry on Mon and Tue).

sections.content: For conditional probability, **always define events** and quote P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B). Venn diagrams help for two-event problems.
